patterns.comment: date literal, e.g. 2025-01-01 -- must come before a plain number match
patterns.name: constant.numeric.date.nuclescript

confirmation-keywords.comment: confirm/hardware/physical_key gate every Synthesis/Sequencing/Destructive effect -- given a distinct scope so an editor theme can make them visually stand out from ordinary control flow, matching how load-bearing they are in the language's safety model. The parser matches keywords case-insensitively (eq_ignore_ascii_case), but every real example uses lowercase consistently -- matching that convention case-sensitively (rather than mirroring the parser's leniency) is what avoids 'strand' the keyword and 'Strand' the type name (and similarly seq/Sequence, recovery/Recovery) colliding on the exact same word.

profile-constants.comment: Profile::parse (ast.rs) also accepts lowercase-hyphenated aliases -- included alongside the PascalCase forms every example actually uses

codec-constants.comment: Codec::parse (ast.rs) also accepts lowercase-hyphenated aliases -- included alongside the PascalCase forms every example actually uses

patterns.comment: multi-character comparison forms must be listed as alternatives here (not as separate, later patterns) so they win over the single-character '<'/'>' match at the same position
patterns.name: keyword.operator.comparison.nuclescript

patterns.comment: &&/||/! -- '!' alone only matches here when it isn't part of '!=', which the comparison pattern above already claims
patterns.name: keyword.operator.logical.nuclescript

patterns.comment: match arm separator -- listed before the plain '=' assignment pattern below so '=>' is matched as one token, not '=' followed by a separately-scoped '>'
patterns.name: keyword.operator.match-arm.nuclescript

patterns.comment: postfix Result-unwrap/propagate operator (Step 9) -- its own scope since it's semantically distinct enough from &&/||/! to deserve separate editor theming, matching how confirmation-keywords already gets its own scope over ordinary control-keywords for the same reason
patterns.name: keyword.operator.try.nuclescript

patterns.comment: an identifier immediately followed by '(' -- covers both user-declared function calls and the consensus_vote builtin (already scoped as a control keyword above, so this only fires for user calls since control-keywords is matched first in the patterns list)
patterns.name: entity.name.function.nuclescript
